***The Recruit*** is an American [spy](/wiki/Spy_fiction "Spy fiction") [adventure](/wiki/Adventure_fiction "Adventure fiction") television series created by Alexi Hawley for [Netflix](/wiki/Netflix "Netflix"). The show follows Owen Hendricks ([Noah Centineo](/wiki/Noah_Centineo "Noah Centineo")), a [CIA lawyer](/wiki/Organizational_structure_of_the_Central_Intelligence_Agency "Organizational structure of the Central Intelligence Agency") who becomes involved in massive international conflicts with dangerous parties after an [asset](/wiki/Asset_(intelligence) "Asset (intelligence)") tries to expose her relationship to the agency.

The series premiered on December 16, 2022, and the second season was released on January 30, 2025. In March 2025, *The Recruit* was canceled after two seasons.

## Synopsis

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Recruit_(American_TV_series)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Synopsis")]

Fledgling lawyer Owen Hendricks, who has just started working for the [CIA](/wiki/Central_Intelligence_Agency "Central Intelligence Agency") after graduating from [Georgetown Law](/wiki/Georgetown_University_Law_Center "Georgetown University Law Center"), has his life turned upside down when he encounters an Eastern European [asset](/wiki/Asset_(intelligence) "Asset (intelligence)") who threatens [extortion](/wiki/Extortion "Extortion") if she is not freed from prison. As her long-term relationship with the CIA is revealed, he becomes entangled in convoluted international politics. Hendricks negotiates with the asset and finds himself at odds with menacing individuals and groups, risking his life as he tries to fulfill his duties.

## Cast and characters

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Recruit_(American_TV_series)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Cast and characters")]

### Main

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Recruit_(American_TV_series)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Main")]

* [Noah Centineo](/wiki/Noah_Centineo "Noah Centineo") as Owen Hendricks, a newly hired CIA lawyer
* [Laura Haddock](/wiki/Laura_Haddock "Laura Haddock") as Max Meladze (season 1), a former CIA asset who is in prison for beating a shady trucker to death
* [Aarti Mann](/wiki/Aarti_Mann "Aarti Mann") as Violet Ebner,[[1]](#cite_note-S1_Main_cast-2) Owen's co-worker who messes with him
* [Colton Dunn](/wiki/Colton_Dunn "Colton Dunn") as Lester Kitchens,[[1]](#cite_note-S1_Main_cast-2) another co-worker of Owen's, and Violet's partner in crime
* [Fivel Stewart](/wiki/Fivel_Stewart "Fivel Stewart") as Hannah Copeland,[[1]](#cite_note-S1_Main_cast-2) Owen's roommate, ex-girlfriend, and former law school classmate
* Daniel Quincy Annoh as Terence Hoffman[[1]](#cite_note-S1_Main_cast-2) (season 1; guest season 2), Owen's other roommate and friend
* [Kristian Bruun](/wiki/Kristian_Bruun "Kristian Bruun") as Janus Ferber, Owen's overstressed colleague who reluctantly helps him out
* [Vondie Curtis-Hall](/wiki/Vondie_Curtis-Hall "Vondie Curtis-Hall") as Walter Nyland, Owen's boss and the CIA's general counsel
* Kaylah Zander as Amelia Salazar (season 2; recurring season 1), another colleague of Owen's, who also dates him
* [Maddie Hasson](/wiki/Maddie_Hasson "Maddie Hasson") as Nichka Lashin (season 2; guest season 1), a criminal and Max Meladze's grown daughter
* [Angel Parker](/wiki/Angel_Parker "Angel Parker") as Dawn Gilbane (season 2; recurring season 1), CIA operative doing a [black op](/wiki/Black_op "Black op") in Yemen
* [Teo Yoo](/wiki/Teo_Yoo "Teo Yoo") as Jang Kyu (season 2),[[2]](#cite_note-TeoY-3)[[3]](#cite_note-S2Recurr9-4) a South Korean [NIS](/wiki/National_Intelligence_Service_(South_Korea) "National Intelligence Service (South Korea)") agent

### Recurring

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Recruit_(American_TV_series)&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Recurring")]

* [Victor Andrés Trelles Turgeon](/wiki/Victor_Andr%C3%A9s_Trelles_Turgeon "Victor Andrés Trelles Turgeon") as Talco
* [Byron Mann](/wiki/Byron_Mann "Byron Mann") as Xander Goi, a senior case officer
* [Nathan Fillion](/wiki/Nathan_Fillion "Nathan Fillion") as Alton West,[[4]](#cite_note-5) CIA director[[5]](#cite_note-6)

## Production

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Recruit_(American_TV_series)&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Production")]

### Development

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Recruit_(American_TV_series)&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Development")]

It was announced that Netflix acquired an unnamed espionage drama on April 28, 2021, with [Entertainment One](/wiki/Entertainment_One_Television "Entertainment One Television") backing the production.[[9]](#cite_note-:0-10)[[10]](#cite_note-:1-11)[[11]](#cite_note-:6-12) Dubbed *Graymail*, the project was created by Alexi Hawley, executive producer of several popular [procedural drama](/wiki/Procedural_drama "Procedural drama") series, such as *[Castle](/wiki/Castle_(TV_series) "Castle (TV series)")*, *[The Rookie](/wiki/The_Rookie_(TV_series) "The Rookie (TV series)")*, and [its spin-off](/wiki/The_Rookie:_Feds "The Rookie: Feds").[[9]](#cite_note-:0-10)[[10]](#cite_note-:1-11)[[12]](#cite_note-:2-13)[[13]](#cite_note-:12-14) Hawley again served as [executive producer](/wiki/Executive_producer_(television) "Executive producer (television)") as well as [showrunner](/wiki/Showrunner "Showrunner"). Alongside Hawley, Noah Centineo; [Doug Liman](/wiki/Doug_Liman "Doug Liman"), Gene Klein, and [Dave Bartis](/wiki/Dave_Bartis "Dave Bartis") of [Hypnotic](/wiki/Hypnotic_(production_company) "Hypnotic (production company)"); and [Adam Ciralsky](/wiki/Adam_Ciralsky "Adam Ciralsky") and [Charlie Ebersol](/wiki/Charlie_Ebersol "Charlie Ebersol") of [P3 Media](/wiki/Adam_Ciralsky "Adam Ciralsky") also joined the project as executive producers.[[14]](#cite_note-:8-15)[[15]](#cite_note-:3-16)[[16]](#cite_note-:4-17) The project was set as an eight-part one-hour television series.[[11]](#cite_note-:6-12)[[15]](#cite_note-:3-16)[[17]](#cite_note-:9-18) It was unveiled on September 28, 2022, that Hawley, George Ghanem, Amelia Roper, Hadi Deeb, Niceole Levy, and Maya Goldsmith served as the series writers. Meanwhile, Doug Liman, [Alex Kalymnios](/wiki/Alex_Kalymnios "Alex Kalymnios"), Emmanuel Osei-Kuffour Jr., and Julian Holmes directed the episodes.[[18]](#cite_note-:11-19) On January 26, 2023, Netflix renewed the series for a second season.[[19]](#cite_note-S2Renewal-20) On March 5, 2025, Netflix canceled the series after two seasons.[[20]](#cite_note-21)

### Casting

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Recruit_(American_TV_series)&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Casting")]

When the production was announced on April 28, 2021, Centineo was unveiled as the lead character of the series.[[9]](#cite_note-:0-10)[[11]](#cite_note-:6-12) More castings were revealed on November 12, 2021, with Aarti Mann, Daniel Quincy Annoh, Vondie Curtis Hall, Kristian Bruun, Laura Haddock, Colton Dunn, and Fivel Stewart joining the main cast.[[14]](#cite_note-:8-15)[[15]](#cite_note-:3-16)[[17]](#cite_note-:9-18)[[21]](#cite_note-:7-22) In addition, Byron Mann, Angel Parker, and Kaylah Zander were also announced in recurring capacities.[[14]](#cite_note-:8-15)[[15]](#cite_note-:3-16)

On December 19, 2023, [Teo Yoo](/wiki/Teo_Yoo "Teo Yoo") was cast as a new series regular for the second season.[[2]](#cite_note-TeoY-3) On January 25, 2024, [James Purefoy](/wiki/James_Purefoy "James Purefoy"), [Brooke Smith](/wiki/Brooke_Smith_(actress) "Brooke Smith (actress)"), [Devika Bhise](/wiki/Devika_Bhise "Devika Bhise"), [Felix Solis](/wiki/Felix_Solis "Felix Solis"), Young-Ah Kim, [Do Hyun Shin](/wiki/Shin_Do-hyun "Shin Do-hyun"), [Sanghee Lee](/wiki/Lee_Sang-hee_(actress) "Lee Sang-hee (actress)"), [Omar Maskati](/wiki/Omar_Maskati "Omar Maskati"), and [Alana Hawley Purvis](/wiki/Alana_Hawley_Purvis "Alana Hawley Purvis") joined the cast in recurring capacities for the second season.[[3]](#cite_note-S2Recurr9-4)

### Filming

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Recruit_(American_TV_series)&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Filming")]

[Principal photography](/wiki/Principal_photography "Principal photography") for the first season took place in [Sorel-Tracy](/wiki/Sorel-Tracy "Sorel-Tracy"), Quebec, and [Los Angeles](/wiki/Los_Angeles "Los Angeles"), California, on October 25, 2021,[[12]](#cite_note-:2-13)[[13]](#cite_note-:12-14) and concluded on March 28, 2022.[[22]](#cite_note-:5-23)[*[better source needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability#Questionable_sources "Wikipedia:Verifiability")*] Filming for the second season began on January 4, 2024, and took place in [Vancouver](/wiki/Vancouver "Vancouver"), Canada, and [Seoul](/wiki/Seoul "Seoul"), South Korea.[[23]](#cite_note-24)

## Release

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Recruit_(American_TV_series)&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Release")]

*The Recruit* premiered globally on Netflix on December 16, 2022.[[16]](#cite_note-:4-17) The second season was released on January 30, 2025.[[24]](#cite_note-25)

### Marketing

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Recruit_(American_TV_series)&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Marketing")]

The series was promoted during Netflix's [TUDUM Global](/wiki/Tudum "Tudum") event on September 24, 2022, on [YouTube](/wiki/YouTube "YouTube").[[21]](#cite_note-:7-22)[[16]](#cite_note-:4-17)

## Reception

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Recruit_(American_TV_series)&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Reception")]

For the first season, the [review aggregator](/wiki/Review_aggregator "Review aggregator") website [Rotten Tomatoes](/wiki/Rotten_Tomatoes "Rotten Tomatoes") reported a 68% approval rating, with an average rating of 7/10, based on 31 critic reviews. The website's critics consensus reads, "*The Recruit* may not be the freshest chip off the old block, but it employs [Noah Centineo](/wiki/Noah_Centineo "Noah Centineo")'s considerable charms to highly watchable effect."[[25]](#cite_note-26) [Metacritic](/wiki/Metacritic "Metacritic"), which uses a weighted average, assigned the show a score of 59 out of 100 based on 13 critics, indicating "mixed or average reviews".[[26]](#cite_note-27)

[Richard Roeper](/wiki/Richard_Roeper "Richard Roeper") of *[Chicago Sun-Times](/wiki/Chicago_Sun-Times "Chicago Sun-Times")* gave the series 3 out of 4 stars and said, "Noah Centineo makes a likable lead on the flashy, funny series."[[27]](#cite_note-28)

On Rotten Tomatoes, the second season has an approval rating of 87%, based on 15 critic reviews, with an average rating of 6.4/10.[[28]](#cite_note-29) Metacritic assigned the second season a score of 73 out of 100, based on 4 critics, indicating "generally favorable" reviews.[[29]](#cite_note-30)
